concentration.headline: Highly distributed urban network
concentration.body: No single city overwhelms Poland's urban landscape. Warsaw holds 9.4% of tracked urban population across 462 cities — characteristic of federal or historically polycentric countries with multiple strong economic centers.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United States, Germany, Switzerland

gravity.headline: Linear city corridor — Warsaw to Łódź
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Warsaw and Łódź (118 km apart, combined population 2,698,642). Cities are connected along a corridor averaging 196 km between top pairs — typical of coastal, river valley, or industrial belt development.

narration.synthesis: Poland shows a distributed urban system with no dominant primate city (Warsaw at 9.4%), with gravitational connections forming a corridor (Warsaw → Łódź, avg 196 km). Urban power is spread across a geographic axis rather than concentrated at a single hub.
